examples.title: Requirements Change Control Process
examples.description: Algorithm for managing requirements changes in a controlled manner
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ChangeControlProcess(changeRequest)
BEGIN
 SUBMIT changeRequest with description and justification
 IMPACT_ANALYSIS(changeRequest)
 
 IF impactAssessment.acceptable THEN
 REVIEW_COMMITTEE_EVALUATE(changeRequest)
 
 IF approved THEN
 UPDATE_requirementsDocument(changeRequest)
 UPDATE_traceabilityMatrix(changeRequest)
 NOTIFY_stakeholders(changeRequest)
 UPDATE_projectSchedule(changeRequest)
 ELSE
 REJECT_changeRequest(changeRequest)
 NOTIFY_requester(rejectionReason)
 END IF
 ELSE
 REJECT_changeRequest(changeRequest)
 NOTIFY_requester("High impact - not approved")
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the systematic process for handling changes to requirements, ensuring all modifications go through proper evaluation, approval, and documentation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - depends on impact analysis complexity

examples.title: Requirements Prioritization using MoSCoW
examples.description: Algorithm to categorize and prioritize requirements
examples.code:
PROCEDURE PrioritizeRequirements(requirementsList)
BEGIN
 initialize lists: mustHave, shouldHave, couldHave, wontHave
 
 FOR EACH requirement IN requirementsList DO
 criticality = EVALUATE_CRITICALITY(requirement)
 
 IF criticality = "mandatory" THEN
 ADD requirement TO mustHave
 ELSE IF criticality = "important" THEN
 ADD requirement TO shouldHave
 ELSE IF criticality = "valuable" THEN
 ADD requirement TO couldHave
 ELSE
 ADD requirement TO wontHave
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN (mustHave, shouldHave, couldHave, wontHave)
 
 // Priority order for implementation: Must → Should → Could
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm shows how requirements are categorized using MoSCoW method for effective prioritization and project planning.

examples.title: Requirements Elicitation Interview Process
examples.description: Structured approach to conducting interview for requirements gathering
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ConductInterview(stakeholder, interviewGuide)
BEGIN
 PREPARE interviewGuide with objectives and questions
 SCHEDULE meeting with stakeholder
 
 BEGIN Interview
 INTRODUCTION(5 min)
 - Explain purpose, agenda, expected duration
 
 WARMUP_QUESTIONS(10 min)
 - Background questions about stakeholder role
 
 CORE_QUESTIONS(30 min)
 - Ask questions from interviewGuide
 - CLARIFY responses if ambiguous
 - DOCUMENT all responses
 
 PROBING_QUESTIONS(10 min)
 - Follow up on interesting points
 - Ask "why" to understand root causes
 
 WRAPUP(5 min)
 - Summary of key points
 - Confirm understanding
 - Ask if anything missed
 END Interview
 
 TRANSCRIBE notes to formal document
 ANALYZE requirements for completeness
 VALIDATE with stakeholder
 
 RETURN extractedRequirements
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode outlines a structured interview process for effective requirements elicitation, including preparation, execution, and documentation phases.
